content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Greg and Graph". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/295/problem/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers n and m. The next m lines contain three integers u, v and w, representing an edge between vertices u and v with weight w.
content.outputFormat: The minimum spanning tree cost of the graph.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use Kruskal's Algorithm to find the minimum spanning tree of the graph.

code.Python3:
import sys
class Edge: def __init__(self, w, u, v): self.w = w; self.u = u; self.v = v
def find(parent, i): if parent[i] == i: return i; return find(parent, parent[i])
def unionSet(parent, rank, x, y): xroot = find(parent, x); yroot = find(parent, y)
if rank[xroot] < rank[yroot]: parent[xroot] = yroot
elif rank[xroot] > rank[yroot]: parent[yroot] = xroot
else: parent[yroot] = xroot; rank[xroot] += 1
n, m = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
parent = list(range(n)); rank = [0] * n
edges = []
for _ in range(m): u, v, w = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()); edges.append(Edge(w, u - 1, v - 1))
edges.sort(key=lambda x: x.w)
result = 0
for edge in edges: if find(parent, edge.u) != find(parent, edge.v): result += edge.w; unionSet(parent, rank, edge.u, edge.v)
print(result)
